risk_title: Insecurity Preventing Project Staff Participation in Implementation Support Mission
risk_summary: Sokoto and Kebbi State Project Implementation Unit representatives were unable to participate in the June 2025 implementation support mission due to unsafe road conditions, limiting direct engagement with World Bank supervision and reducing implementation oversight in these states.
evidence_quote: Sokoto and Kebbi SPIU representatives were unable to join the mission in-person due to lack of flights and unsafe road conditions.

risk_title: Arbitrary Teacher and Principal Transfers Disrupting Project Implementation
risk_summary: Despite directives from state authorities to prevent transfers of teachers and principals from AGILE-supported schools, arbitrary movements continue to occur. This disrupts digital literacy program continuity and creates knowledge gaps when new staff lack proper handover documentation.
evidence_quote: Arbitrary teacher and principal transfers continue despite directives to prevent such movements by state authorities. In response, the Project has planned follow-up training for SPIU procurement staff on World Bank procurement regulations, SPIUs are encouraged to hold regular joint planning sessions and cross-component meetings to align budgets and activities, and SPIUs have been asked to adopt a device tracking system and develop detailed logistics and distribution plans, including timelines and responsible parties.

risk_title: Sexual Exploitation Incident Triggering Safeguards Response
risk_summary: One case of sexual exploitation was reported, triggering the Environment & Social Incident Response Toolkit. While the survivor received support, the incident revealed gaps in the GBV incident reporting system across selected states, indicating systemic vulnerabilities in safeguarding mechanisms.
evidence_quote: One case of sexual exploitation was reported to the task team, triggering the Environment & Social Incident Response Toolkit (ESIRT). Although the survivor received support at the time of the incident, the SPIU commissioned an investigation using a survivor-centered approach to understand the root causes and implement measures to prevent recurrence.

risk_title: Weak Financial Management Controls Creating Fiduciary Risk in Kogi State
risk_summary: Kogi State SPIU has been rated Highly Unsatisfactory for financial management due to weak internal controls, high involvement of project leadership in fiduciary functions, outsourcing of activities without contracts or World Bank approval, and inflated pricing with lack of competitive procurement. These governance failures create substantial risk of ineligible expenditures.
evidence_quote: In Kogi, FM performance was assessed to be Highly Unsatisfactory (HU). The mission noted that the internal control framework at the SPIU is undermined by the high level of involvement of the project leadership in fiduciary functions, creating a high-risk environment and increasing the likelihood of ineligible expenditures. Project activities were outsourced to firms without signed contracts, defined deliverables, or prior World Bank approval.
